claim: A novel HEA lightweight alloy has been discovered that exhibits greater corrosion resistance than the nickel alloys this replaces. It's BCC structure enables >80 % elongation under tension, which is greater than that observed for FCC or mixed FCC and BCC phase HEA alloys.

explanation.text: See Figure 1. All of the BCC phases are significantly to the left

author: apl2

claim: A new B1 superconductor has been discovered with a higher Tc than is observed in the B2 family.

explanation.text: Compare table 5 (B1) and table 6 (B2). B1 superconductors have higher Tc's than B2 superconductors. This is consistent with that trend. One can't evaluate whether the discovery is real, only that it is plausible.

author: apl2

claim: Chromium processed by swaging with 71% reduction and annealing at 1250 °C produced fine equiaxed grains (~95 µm) produces a much better yield strengths performance than chromium that was extruded and annealed at 1595 °C developed much coarser grains (~500 µm).

explanation.text: Table 1, just what the table shows, first Cr entry vs second but simpler

author: apl1

claim: A new qubit made of PtSi exhibits a Tc of 3 K at atmospheric pressure when made under the right conditions.

explanation.text: 1K is typical (Table 9). Ruling out pressure, 3 K becomes extremely unlikely.
Sometimes making nanoscale superconductors can increase Tc. Nanoscale PtSi films do not show any precedence for PtSi films with a 3 K Tc.

author: apl2

claim: A new superconducting device has been developed based around superconducting ScN. This material, when made under the right conditions, has a superconducting transition temperature of 3 K at atmospheric pressure.

explanation.text: Matthias has demonstrated that it does not superconduct down to 1.4 K. (Table 5)
ScN is a degenerately-doped semiconductor so it is unlikely that further doping would introduce superconductivity.

author: apl2
